name: Garlic Potatoes
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel the potatoes.
2. Gently boil in salted water until barely tender. Drain well.
3. Return the drained potatoes to the pan and shake over low heat until they have a light brown coating on the outside.
4. Cool the potatoes, then thinly slice them.
5. Melt the butter in a large skillet.
6. Add the garlic (pressed) and season with salt and pepper.
7. Add the sliced potatoes to the skillet. Gently toss so that the potatoes are evenly coated with butter.
8. Place the skillet on low heat and continue cooking, stirring occasionally, until a nice brown crust forms on the bottom of the potato slices.
9. Serve hot. Makes 6 servings.
Food Exchanges per serving: 1 STARCH/BREAD Exchange + 1 FAT Exchange (If using medium potatoes, about half a cup for the starch exchange; use pan spray and reduce oil to achieve one fat exchange per serving). This is a dish that Bert would enjoy.
Vegetable Cookbook by Paul Mayer and Nitty Gritty Productions
